subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A commercial driver’s license or commercial learner’s permit must:
be marked:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: “Commercial Driver License” or “CDL” for a commercial driver’s license; or

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: “Commercial Learner’s Permit” or “CLP” for a commercial learner’s permit;
be, to the extent practicable, tamper-proof; and
include:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: the name and domicile address of the person to whom it is issued;

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: the person’s photograph;

subsections.label: C
subsections.text: a physical description of the person, including sex, height, and eye color;

subsections.label: D
subsections.text: the person’s date of birth;

subsections.label: E
subsections.text: a number or identifier the department considers appropriate;

subsections.label: F
subsections.text: the person’s signature;

subsections.label: G
subsections.text: each class of commercial motor vehicle that the person is authorized to drive, with any endorsements or restrictions;

subsections.label: H
subsections.text: the name of this state; and

subsections.label: I
subsections.text: the dates between which the license is valid.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: Except as provided by this section, a commercial driver’s license issued under this chapter:
must:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: be in the same format;

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: have the same appearance and orientation; and

subsections.label: C
subsections.text: contain the same type of information; and
may not include any information that this chapter does not reference or require.

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: To the extent of a conflict or inconsistency between this section and Section 522.013 (Non-domiciled License or Permit) or 522.051 (Expiration of License or Permit) , Section 522.013 (Non-domiciled License or Permit) or 522.051 (Expiration of License or Permit) controls.

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: The department shall ensure that an original or renewal commercial driver’s license or commercial learner’s permit issued under this chapter properly records any diacritical mark used in a person’s name. In this subsection, “diacritical mark” means a mark used in Latin script to change the sound of a letter to which it is added or used to distinguish the meaning of the word in which the letter appears. The term includes accents, tildes, graves, umlauts, and cedillas.

history.raw: Acts 2017, 85th Leg., R.S., Ch. 1041 (H.B.1823), Sec. 3, eff. September 1, 2017.
